headline: Orion Embeds Denali AI Into $5.8T Platform: Enterprise Intelligence Layer for 8M Accounts, Anthropic Partnership Expanding
summary: Orion has launched Denali AI Enterprise, an intelligence layer embedded directly into its existing advisor platform that supports $5.8 trillion in assets under administration and 8 million technology accounts. The product includes Report Assistant (client-ready drafts from firm templates) and Query Studio (plain-English data queries for operations teams). Orion serves 17 of the top 20 Barron's RIA firms and is expanding its partnership with Anthropic on financial services capabilities. According to the announcement, only 1 in 10 advisors currently use advanced AI tools (agentic or cross-system), and 23% have no AI tools at all — while 60% say AI-driven efficiency is a top priority for 2026. Additional AI assistants for proposal generation, account opening, billing, and reconciliation are planned for 2026.
the_so_what: Orion embedding AI into a platform that already runs 17 of the top 20 Barron's RIA firms and $5.8T in assets means the adoption question is solved by distribution — advisors don't choose Denali, they get it inside the tools they already use. The Anthropic partnership puts Orion in the same infrastructure camp as LPL (which also expanded with Anthropic in February 2026), creating a de facto Claude-powered layer across a significant share of the independent channel. For standalone advisor AI tools like Jump and Zocks, this is the threat: platform-embedded AI that arrives for free inside existing workflows.
